topic: How AI-Generated Code and AI Agent Commits Affect Git History Analysis and Developer Metrics

sources.title: AI Copilot Code Quality: 2025 Data Suggests 4x Growth in Code Clones - GitClear
keyInsights: 211M lines analyzed from 2020-2024 across major tech companies
Copy/pasted code rose from 8.3% to 12.3%
Refactored/moved code dropped from 25% to under 10%
8x increase in duplicated code blocks
Short-term churn (revised within 2 weeks) rose from 3.1% to 5.7%

sources.title: AI IDEs or Autonomous Agents? Measuring the Impact of Coding Agents on Software Development
keyInsights: Agent-first repos: +36.3% commits, +76.6% lines added
IDE-first repos: +3.06% commits, -6.34% lines added
Cognitive complexity up ~39% regardless of prior AI exposure
Diminishing returns to AI assistance demonstrated
Staggered difference-in-differences methodology with matched controls

sources.title: 2025 AI Metrics in Review: What 12 Months of Data Tell Us - Jellyfish
keyInsights: 50% of companies now have at least 50% AI-generated code
PRs per engineer increased 113% at full AI adoption
Median cycle time dropped 24% (16.7 to 12.7 hours)
Code assistant adoption went from 49.2% to 69% in 2025
Cursor gained from under 20% to nearly 40% market share

sources.title: GitHub Octoverse 2025 Report
keyInsights: 986 million commits pushed in 2025 (+25.1% YoY)
43.2 million PRs merged monthly (+23% YoY)
80% of new developers use Copilot within first week
1.1M+ public repos import an LLM SDK (+178% YoY)
11.5 billion GitHub Actions minutes consumed (+35% YoY)

sources.title: How Git AI Works - Git AI Documentation
keyInsights: Explicit marking system - agents invoke hooks to mark AI code
Git Notes storage indexed by commit SHA
Checkpoint system tracks incremental changes between commit start and end
Preserves attribution across rebases, merges, squashes, cherry-picks
~10-20ms overhead per command, fully offline

sources.title: Agent Trace - Open Specification for AI Code Attribution
keyInsights: JSON-based trace records connecting code ranges to conversations
Categories: human, AI, mixed, unknown
Storage-agnostic - supports files, git notes, databases
Supports multiple VCS including Git, Jujutsu, Mercurial
Currently in RFC/draft phase

sources.title: The New Git Blame: Who's Responsible When AI Writes the Code?
keyInsights: Four responsibility frameworks for AI code
Bot Sponsorship pattern for accountability
Claude's Co-Authored-By email registration issues
Legal consequences in regulated industries
Recommendation to annotate commits with model names and prompts

sources.title: Agentic AI Coding: Best Practice Patterns for Speed with Quality - CodeScene
keyInsights: Six best practice patterns for agentic AI coding
Code Health threshold of 9.5-10.0 for AI-ready code
MCP safeguards expose code quality feedback to agents
AI operates in self-harm mode - writes code it cannot maintain
Multi-level safeguards: continuous review, pre-commit, PR pre-flight

sources.title: AI-Generated Code Looks Clean. Here's Why Your Next Refactor Will Prove It Isn't
keyInsights: 82% of AI PRs had inadequate error handling
76% lacked network timeouts
44% introduced N+1 database query problems
Duplicate code ratios rose from 3.1% to 14.2%
68% of AI modules require complete rewrites, not minor fixes
AI PRs average 10.83 issues vs 6.45 for human code

sources.title: Did AI Erase Attribution? Your Git History Is Missing a Co-Author
keyInsights: Proposed four-level attribution system (Assisted/Co-authored/Generated/Commit-generated)
AI contributions systematically erased from git history
Existing Co-authored-by convention underutilized for AI
Conventional Commits with AI attribution footers recommended

sources.title: Maintaining Code Ownership in the Age of AI Assistance
keyInsights: Disconnect between implied understanding and actual knowledge
Developer responsibility remains regardless of generation method
Knowledge transfer risks from AI-assisted workflows
Recommendation to trace execution paths and verify edge cases

sources.title: The Rise of Vibe Coding: Why Architecture Still Matters in the Age of AI Agents
keyInsights: AI agents generate architecture by default without guidance
Missing abstraction layers unless specifically instructed
Agents skip services, repositories, DTOs by default
Architecture-aware prompting strategies recommended
vFunction detects coupling violations in agent-generated changes

sources.title: Auto-Detect and Review AI-Generated Code from GitHub Copilot - Sonar
keyInsights: Auto-detects Copilot usage via GitHub API
Routes AI code through AI Code Assurance quality gates
Built-in Sonar way for AI Code with seven conditions
Projects passing gate receive quality badge

sources.title: How to Use Git with Claude Code: Understanding the Co-Authored-By Attribution
keyInsights: Claude Code adds Co-Authored-By trailer by default
Configurable via includeCoAuthoredBy and gitAttribution settings
Claude won't appear in main contributors list unless directly authored
Project-level overrides via .claude/settings.json

sources.title: Agent Trace: Cursor Proposes an Open Specification for AI Code Attribution - InfoQ
keyInsights: Agent Trace addresses gap where git blame cannot distinguish AI from human
Currently in RFC phase with cautiously optimistic developer response
Leaves merge/rebase handling as unresolved question
Does not define ownership semantics or quality assessment

sources.title: How AI-Generated Code is Messing with Your Technical Debt
keyInsights: Code churn doubled between 2021 and 2024
DORA report: 7.2% drop in software delivery stability linked to AI
90% of developers have committed AI-generated code
30% average acceptance rate for Copilot suggestions
Treat AI as ultra-fast intern requiring guidance

sources.title: The Rise of Git Worktrees in the Age of AI Coding Agents
keyInsights: Worktree farm model: multiple agents work simultaneously
Shared git object database eliminates duplication
Each worktree maps to distinct branch for isolation
Development pipelines resemble distributed agent systems

sources.title: How AI-Generated Code Compounds Technical Debt - LeadDev
keyInsights: AI generates similar solutions repeatedly rather than refactoring
Volume of generated code exceeds team review capacity
Velocity improvements negated by maintenance overhead
Coding standards needed before AI adoption

sources.title: What 986 Million Code Pushes Say About Developer Workflow in 2025 - GitHub Blog
keyInsights: Smaller and more frequent commits becoming the norm
Feature flags now central to deployment strategy
11.5 billion Actions minutes (+35% YoY)
PR reviews trending toward smaller single-purpose reviews

sources.title: Coding on Copilot: 2023 Data Suggests Downward Pressure on Code Quality - GitClear
keyInsights: First major study linking Copilot to code quality decline
2024 projections for continued quality erosion
Established methodology for measuring AI code impact
Foundation for the 2025 follow-up study

sources.title: Git AI - A Git Extension for Tracking AI-Generated Code
keyInsights: Open source zero-configuration CLI tool
Vendor-agnostic AI code tracking
Tags AI code at moment of creation
Preserves metadata through entire git workflow

sources.title: GitHub Hits Nearly 1 Billion Commits in a Year - AI Is Rewriting Software Development
keyInsights: Growing share of commit activity reflects agentic AI workflows
Autonomous agents create PRs without direct human code authorship
36 million new developers joined GitHub in one year

sources.title: AI-Generated Code Quality Metrics and Statistics for 2026
keyInsights: AI introduces 1.7x more total issues than human code
15-18% more security vulnerabilities in AI code
71% do not merge AI code without manual review
Only 3% highly trust AI-generated code

sources.title: Vibe Coding Statistics: Productivity, Risk in AI-Assisted Development
keyInsights: PR size increased 154% on average with AI
Review times balloon 91% with high AI adoption
Code review time increases 25-40% for AI code
Security issues 2.74x higher in AI-generated code

sources.title: Behavioral Code Analysis - CodeScene
keyInsights: Combines static analysis with behavioral VCS data
CodeHealth metric links quality to development velocity
Hotspot analysis identifies frequently-changed unhealthy code
Development Output normalized by contributing authors
